tldr.summary: Implement a comprehensive gate architecture for the Development Swarm covering pre-execution, post-execution, and between-wave validation. Extract 7 reusable components from the planning harness into `internal/gate/`. Add Junior Engineer Protection with three-layer TDD enforcement. Define output and behavioural agent gates across four categories (Development, Security, Compliance, Resource).
tldr.deliverables: Gate infrastructure package (`internal/gate/`), Junior Engineer protection gates, between-wave validators, updated agent manifests, gate examples, full test coverage.
tldr.estimated_effort: Complex — 5 phases, 18 tasks across 10 execution waves.
tldr.parallel_execution: Wave 2 tasks (T4, T5, T6) run concurrently; Wave 4 tasks (T11, T12) run concurrently; Wave 7 tasks (T15, T16) run concurrently.
tldr.critical_path: T1 → T2 → T3 → T4 → T7 → T10 → T13 → T16 (foundation through full integration).

context.original_request: Improve gate checks in the Development Swarm and define quality gates for agents with pre-execution, post-execution, and between-wave gate infrastructure. Extract 7 reusable components from the planning harness. Cover four gate categories: Development, Security, Compliance, Resource. Implement Junior Engineer Protection (pre-block, post-validate, TDD redirection). Define output and behavioural agent gates.

research_findings.langgraph_checkpoints: Persistent state management via state snapshotting and rollback; pattern applicable to gate state persistence across waves
research_findings.crewai_monitoring: Task-level metrics, crew-level coordination gates, real-time feedback loops
research_findings.harbor_validator_framework: Pluggable validator chains, evaluate-retry loops, capability checks — directly maps to our 7-component extraction

architectural_decisions.rationale: Centralised, importable by any agent or orchestrator
Standardised PASS/FAIL/SKIP verdict across all gates
Composable validation pipelines for different contexts
Existing component adapted for post-execution assessment
Inspired by LangGraph; enables rollback and audit trails

objectives.core_objective: Build a production-quality gate infrastructure that enforces quality standards across the Development Swarm lifecycle — before agents execute, after they produce output, and between execution waves.
deliverables: Pre-execution gate implementations (capability, resource, security, TDD discipline)
Post-execution gate implementations (output quality, behavioural compliance)
Between-wave gate implementations (wave completion, state consistency, readiness)
Agent manifest updates with gate requirements
Working examples demonstrating gates in action
definition_of_done: All 7 reusable components extracted and unit-tested in `internal/gate/`
Junior Engineer Protection gates block invalid execution and redirect to TDD
Between-wave gates validate wave completion before next wave starts
All four gate categories have at least one implemented gate
BDD test suite covers all gate paths at ≥95% coverage
Existing 224 unit tests + 5 BDD tests remain green
must_have: Junior Engineer pre-block, post-validate, and TDD redirection
must_not_have: Synchronous blocking calls that violate async delegation pattern
Removal or modification of existing passing tests
External dependencies not already in the project

test_approach.bdd_tests: `godog` for gate scenario coverage (gate-blocked, gate-passed, gate-redirected)
test_approach.integration_tests: End-to-end gate execution within swarm wave simulation
test_approach.regression: All existing 224 unit tests + 5 BDD tests must remain green

qa_policy.acceptance_criteria: Every task includes explicit acceptance criteria and QA scenarios
qa_policy.dedicated_qa: A dedicated QA pass runs after each phase before the next begins
qa_policy.performance_overhead: Gate performance overhead measured and must not exceed 5% of wave execution time
qa_policy.false_positive_rate: False-positive rate tracked: target <2% of gate blocks should be overridden

parallel_waves.notes: T7 first, then T8 + T9 concurrent
T10 first, then T11 + T12 concurrent
T16 first, then T17 + T18 concurrent

agent_dispatch.rationale: Core infrastructure requires deep architectural understanding
Integration points need both implementation and verification

tasks.title: Extract and implement ValidationResult core type
tasks.description: Create the `ValidationResult` struct in `internal/gate/` as the standardised verdict structure for all gates. Must support PASS, FAIL, SKIP states with reason, metadata, and timestamp fields.
acceptance_criteria: `ValidationResult` struct with `Status` (PASS/FAIL/SKIP), `Reason string`, `Metadata map[string]any`, `Timestamp time.Time`
Constructor functions: `NewPassResult(reason string)`, `NewFailResult(reason string)`, `NewSkipResult(reason string)`
`IsPass()`, `IsFail()`, `IsSkip()` helper methods
`String()` method for human-readable output
Unit tests covering all states and constructors
No external dependencies beyond standard library

tasks.title: Implement combineValidationResults multi-validator aggregation
tasks.description: Create the `combineValidationResults` function that aggregates multiple `ValidationResult` instances into a single verdict. Implements short-circuit FAIL semantics.
acceptance_criteria: `CombineValidationResults(results ...ValidationResult) ValidationResult` function
All PASS → combined PASS
Any FAIL → combined FAIL (short-circuit)
All SKIP → combined SKIP
Mixed PASS+SKIP → combined PASS with skip metadata
Mixed FAIL+anything → combined FAIL with all reasons aggregated

tasks.title: Extract buildFeedback and appendFeedback helpers
tasks.description: Extract and generalise the `buildFeedback` and `appendFeedback` functions from the planning harness. These construct structured feedback messages when gates fail.
acceptance_criteria: `Feedback` struct with `Category` (Error/Warning/Suggestion), `Message string`, `Context map[string]string`, `Timestamp time.Time`
`BuildFeedback(category, message string) *Feedback` constructor
`AppendFeedback(existing *Feedback, category, message string) *Feedback` chaining method
`FeedbackString()` method produces formatted multi-line output

tasks.title: Implement ValidatorChain sequential validation pipeline
tasks.description: Create the `ValidatorChain` that executes a sequence of validators in order. Supports both short-circuit and full-execution modes.
acceptance_criteria: `Validator` interface: `Validate(ctx context.Context) ValidationResult`
`ValidatorChain` struct with `AddValidator(v Validator)`, `SetMode(mode ChainMode)` (ShortCircuit / FullExecution)
`Execute(ctx context.Context) ValidationResult` runs all validators per mode

tasks.title: Implement CriticResult for behavioural gate assessment
tasks.description: Create the `CriticResult` type adapted from existing planning harness patterns. This type captures behavioural assessment of agent output.
acceptance_criteria: `CriticResult` struct with `Score float64` (0-1), `Issues []string`, `Positives []string`, `Recommendation string`, `Passed bool`
`NewCriticResult(score float64) *CriticResult` constructor
`AddIssue(issue string)`, `AddPositive(positive string)` methods
`ToValidationResult() ValidationResult` conversion method (PASS if score ≥ threshold, default 0.7)

tasks.title: Implement Aggregator for cross-wave state analysis
tasks.description: Create the `Aggregator` component that collects and analyses gate results across multiple waves. Supports trend detection and wave comparison.
acceptance_criteria: `Aggregator` struct with `RecordWave(waveID string, results []ValidationResult)`, `GetWaveResults(waveID string) []ValidationResult`
`CumulativePassRate() float64` — overall pass rate across all recorded waves
`Trend() TrendDirection` (Improving / Declining / Stable) — compares last 3 waves

tasks.title: Implement evaluate-retry quality enforcement loop
tasks.description: Create the `EvaluateRetryLoop` — the core enforcement mechanism that cycles agents back through validation when they fail. Powers the Junior Engineer TDD redirection.
acceptance_criteria: `EvaluateRetryLoop` struct with `SetMaxRetries(n int)`, `SetValidator(v Validator)`, `SetOnRetry(fn func(attempt int, result ValidationResult))`
`Execute(ctx context.Context, executor func() error) ValidationResult`
Runs executor, validates output, retries on FAIL up to max retries
`RetryCount() int` returns actual retry count
`History() []ValidationResult` returns all attempt results

tasks.title: Implement Junior Engineer pre-block capability gate
tasks.description: Create the pre-block layer that validates Junior Engineer readiness before task assignment. Checks: capability assessment, TDD discipline verification, resource availability.
acceptance_criteria: `PreBlockGate` struct implementing `Validator` interface
Capability check: validates agent has required skills for task
TDD discipline check: verifies a failing test exists before implementation can proceed
Resource check: confirms context window, tool availability

tasks.title: Implement TDD execution guard with RED-GREEN-REFACTOR enforcement
tasks.description: Create the runtime TDD enforcement layer that integrates with `sisyphus-junior` prompt enforcement. Monitors execution state (RED → GREEN → REFACTOR).
acceptance_criteria: `TDDGuard` struct with `SetPhase(phase TDDPhase)` (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)
`ValidateTransition(from, to TDDPhase) ValidationResult` — enforces valid transitions
RED → GREEN: only minimum implementation allowed
GREEN → REFACTOR: only under GREEN status
REFACTOR → GREEN: must maintain all passing tests

tasks.title: Implement Junior Engineer post-validate output assessment
tasks.description: Create the post-validate layer that assesses Junior Engineer output after execution completes. Validates: output quality, TDD behavioural compliance, generates improvement feedback.
acceptance_criteria: `PostValidateGate` struct implementing `Validator` interface
Output quality check: verifies produced code meets test specifications
TDD compliance check: verifies agent followed RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle
Test coverage check: verifies adequate test coverage (configurable threshold)

tasks.title: Implement wave completion validation gate
tasks.description: Create the gate that validates whether a wave's deliverables are complete before the next wave begins. Checks: all tasks have results, all results pass quality threshold.
acceptance_criteria: `WaveCompletionGate` struct implementing `Validator` interface
`SetWaveSpec(spec WaveSpec)` configures expected tasks and their validators
Validates all tasks in wave spec have corresponding results
Validates all task results meet quality threshold
Detects orphaned tasks (results for tasks not in spec)

tasks.title: Implement state consistency and readiness gates
tasks.description: Create two related gates: (1) State Consistency Gate — verifies swarm state integrity between waves; (2) Readiness Gate — confirms the swarm has sufficient resources and context to proceed.
acceptance_criteria: `StateConsistencyGate` implementing `Validator`: validates checkpoint integrity, state transition log, detects missing/duplicate checkpoints
`ReadinessGate` implementing `Validator`: checks resource availability, validates required context, checks agent availability

tasks.title: Implement Development and Security category gates
tasks.description: Create concrete gate implementations for the Development and Security categories. Development gates: code quality, test coverage, build success. Security gates: dependency vulnerability scan, secrets detection, permission validation.
acceptance_criteria: Development gates: `CodeQualityGate`, `TestCoverageGate`, `BuildSuccessGate`
Security gates: `DependencyScanGate`, `SecretsDetectionGate`, `PermissionGate`
All gates implement `Validator` interface
Each gate composable into `ValidatorChain` pipelines

tasks.title: Implement Compliance and Resource category gates
tasks.description: Create concrete gate implementations for the Compliance and Resource categories. Compliance gates: coding standards adherence, documentation requirements, licensing checks. Resource gates: compute budget, context window, tool availability.
acceptance_criteria: Compliance gates: `CodingStandardsGate`, `DocumentationGate`, `LicensingGate`
Resource gates: `ComputeBudgetGate`, `ContextWindowGate`, `ToolAvailabilityGate`
All gates implement `Validator` interface

tasks.title: Update agent manifests with gate requirements
tasks.description: Update all agent manifest files to reference the gates they must pass. Each manifest specifies pre-execution, post-execution, and behavioural gates appropriate to the agent's role.
acceptance_criteria: Each agent manifest has `gates` section with `pre_execution`, `post_execution`, `behavioural` subsections
Junior Engineer manifest references pre-block, TDD guard, post-validate gates
Senior Engineer manifest references capability and review gates
QA manifest references quality threshold and coverage gates
`GateManifestLoader` reads gate configuration from manifests

tasks.title: Integrate gates into swarm orchestrator execution flow
tasks.description: Wire all gates into the swarm orchestrator's execution flow. Pre-execution gates run before each agent starts. Post-execution gates run after each agent completes. Between-wave gates run between wave transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator calls pre-execution gate chain before dispatching each agent
Orchestrator calls post-execution gate chain after each agent completes
Orchestrator calls between-wave gate chain between wave transitions
Gate failures produce appropriate handling: block execution, redirect agent, or abort wave
Gate results logged for audit trail
Async-only: all gate checks are non-blocking from the orchestrator's perspective

tasks.title: Create BDD test suite achieving ≥95% coverage
tasks.description: Write a comprehensive BDD test suite using `godog` that covers all gate scenarios. Target ≥95% coverage across `internal/gate/`. Verify all existing 224 unit tests + 5 BDD tests remain green.
acceptance_criteria: Feature files covering all 5 gate domains
Each feature has ≥3 scenarios (happy path, failure, edge case)
Step definitions for all Given/When/Then patterns
`go test ./internal/gate/... -cover` shows ≥95% coverage
All existing tests (`go test ./...`) pass without modification

tasks.title: Create gate examples and comprehensive documentation
tasks.description: Write working examples demonstrating gates in action and comprehensive documentation. Examples: Junior Engineer blocked, Junior Engineer redirected to TDD, QA gate passed, wave transition validated.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture document covers: component overview, data flow, gate lifecycle
Configuration guide covers: manifest format, gate parameters, thresholds
Extension guide covers: implementing custom `Validator`, adding new gate categories
Integration guide covers: wiring gates into orchestrators, agent-delegation-swarm compat
Each example is a runnable Go test (`go test ./examples/gates/...`)

risk_register.mitigation: Performance benchmarks in T18; lazy evaluation for SKIP gates; caching for repeated checks

risk_register.risk: Overly strict gates produce false positives, blocking valid work
risk_register.mitigation: Configurable thresholds; gate override mechanism for senior agents; false-positive rate tracking

risk_register.risk: State checkpoint corruption causes wave inconsistencies
risk_register.mitigation: Hash verification in StateConsistencyGate; automatic rollback to last good checkpoint; audit trail

risk_register.risk: Gate system complexity makes maintenance difficult
risk_register.mitigation: Clear separation of concerns (7 components); comprehensive documentation; BDD test suite as living spec
Gates enforced at orchestrator level (not agent level); behavioural gates detect circumvention patterns; audit logging

risk_register.risk: Poor gate feedback fails to improve agent performance
risk_register.mitigation: Structured feedback via buildFeedback/appendFeedback; specific actionable guidance; feedback quality metrics

risk_register.risk: Breaking changes to existing 224 unit tests + 5 BDD tests
risk_register.mitigation: Regression gate runs full test suite; no modification of existing test files; T17 verifies compatibility

risk_register.risk: Integration with agent-delegation-swarm Phase II causes conflicts
risk_register.mitigation: Explicit compatibility checks in T16; shared gate interface; coordination with agent-delegation-swarm chain

foundation_verification: Each component has unit tests passing independently
No external dependencies beyond standard library and existing project deps
junior_engineer_protection_verification: Pre-block gate blocks agents without required capabilities
Pre-block gate blocks agents without failing test (TDD discipline)
TDD guard enforces RED → GREEN → REFACTOR transitions
TDD guard redirects agents to RED phase on violation
Post-validate gate assesses TDD behavioural compliance
Post-validate gate generates actionable improvement feedback
between_wave_and_integration_verificatio: Wave completion gate detects incomplete and orphaned tasks
State consistency gate detects checkpoint corruption
Readiness gate checks resource and context availability
All 4 gate categories (Development, Security, Compliance, Resource) implemented
Agent manifests reference correct gate chains per agent type
Swarm orchestrator calls gates at correct lifecycle points
quality_and_documentation_verification: All existing 224 unit tests + 5 BDD tests pass unmodified
BDD feature files cover all 5 gate domains
All example tests are runnable (`go test ./examples/gates/...`)
Architecture, configuration, extension, and integration docs complete
Gate performance overhead < 5% of wave execution time
